title: Corrective fix for B-66a74207: Review planner and scheduler changes for feature coherence
description:
Blocked reason: Review scope is undefined because both `touched_files` and `changed_files` are empty; the review template forbids reading unrelated files or approving without evidence.

Remaining work: Populate this bead's `touched_files` and/or `changed_files` with the actual modified planner, prompt, scheduler, and test files, then rerun review against that bounded scope.
acceptance_criteria: Implement the minimum fix required to unblock B-66a74207.
Update tests/docs as needed for the corrective change.
Leave a handoff summary that states how the parent bead can be retried.

handoff_summary.completed: Updated corrective planning in `scheduler.py` so new corrective beads inherit a bounded file set from `touched_files`, then `changed_files`, then `expected_files`. Added a regression test covering the blocked review case with only `expected_files` populated. Verified syntax with `uv run python -m py_compile src/codex_orchestrator/scheduler.py tests/test_orchestrator.py`.
handoff_summary.remaining: Retry the parent review flow after this patch is available so any newly planned corrective bead carries the bounded planner/prompt/scheduler/test scope instead of empty review scope metadata.
handoff_summary.risks: Existing corrective beads that were already created before this patch keep their prior metadata; they may still need to be recreated or manually updated if they already have empty scope fields.
handoff_summary.next_action: Retry the blocked parent review/corrective flow so scope metadata is regenerated with populated `touched_files`/`changed_files`.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: Downstream review/tester agents should assume only newly created corrective beads get the fallback scope automatically; older blocked artifacts may still have empty scope metadata until retried or recreated.

conflict_risks: Downstream review/tester agents should assume only newly created corrective beads get the fallback scope automatically; older blocked artifacts may still have empty scope metadata until retried or recreated.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `uv run python -c "import codex_orchestrator"` or `uv run python -m py_compile <file>`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not `unittest discover`, not `unittest tests.<module>`, not any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
